skill.name: frontend-patterns
skill.description: Frontend development patterns for React, Next.js, state management, performance optimization, forms, accessibility, animation, and UI best practices.
skill.summary: Apply reusable React and Next.js frontend patterns for maintainable, accessible, and performant interfaces.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic because Markdown examples contain dynamic imports, fetch calls, template literals, and key-related words. Manual review found a single documentation-only SKILL.md with React and Next.js examples, no executable scripts, no credential access, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of malicious intent. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from copyable frontend examples that should still be adapted carefully by users.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited lines are TypeScript Markdown code fences and React examples, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows fenced TypeScript examples and JSX template literals. No shell invocation, subprocess API, or executable skill script is present.

low_findings.title: React Lazy Import Examples Only
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings at lines 315 and 316 are false positives. They demonstrate React lazy loading for local components and do not load remote code or execute untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports use static relative module paths inside a documentation example. There is no variable-controlled import target or runtime loader in the skill.

low_findings.title: Local Data Fetching Examples Only
low_findings.description: The fetch findings are low-risk examples for frontend data loading. The examples call a passed URL or a local /api/markets route, with no hardcoded external endpoint or credential transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both calls appear inside educational React examples. No secret access, external host, beaconing, or persistence behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Keyword Collisions in TypeScript Examples
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by words such as description, Object.keys, and KeyboardEvent key handling in UI examples. No certificate, private key, hash algorithm, or cryptographic operation appears in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary UI and validation code, not certificate material or weak cryptography. The detected terms are substring matches in documentation examples.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are UI Code Collisions
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are false positives. The cited lines are React context, button class selection, callback options, and error handling examples, not host or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain frontend component state and callbacks only. No filesystem, OS, process, environment, or network reconnaissance behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need repeatable patterns for components, state, forms, performance, animation, and accessibility. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical React and Next.js examples that guide implementation decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains React composition, compound components, and render props with TypeScript examples.
Provides custom hook patterns for toggles, async queries, and debounced input.
Shows state management with context, reducers, and typed actions.
Demonstrates performance patterns such as memoization, lazy loading, and virtualized lists.
Covers controlled forms, validation, error boundaries, animation, keyboard navigation, and focus management.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not inspect or modify a project by itself.
Examples use generic market data names and may need domain-specific changes.
It does not replace framework documentation or production security review.
It assumes React and TypeScript familiarity for best results.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the composition and compound component examples to align reusable UI patterns across a React codebase.

use_cases.title: Improve App Performance
use_cases.description: Apply memoization, lazy loading, and virtualization guidance to reduce rendering work in complex interfaces.

use_cases.title: Review Accessibility Patterns
use_cases.description: Use keyboard navigation and focus management examples when designing dropdowns, dialogs, and interactive widgets.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-patterns to recommend a React component pattern for this UI: [describe the UI]. Explain why it fits.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-patterns to refactor this React component toward composition or compound components: [paste component].

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Rendering
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-patterns to review this screen for memoization, lazy loading, and list virtualization opportunities: [paste code or describe screen].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Frontend Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-patterns to propose component, hook, state, form, error, and accessibility patterns for this React feature: [feature brief].

output_examples.input: I need a reusable tab interface in React.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a compound component structure with shared context, a tab list, tab buttons, and controlled active state.

output_examples.input: My market list renders slowly with thousands of rows.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend virtualizing the list, estimating row height, limiting rendered items, and memoizing row components.

output_examples.input: I am building a modal and want it to be accessible.
output_examples.output: The skill would highlight dialog roles, Escape handling, initial focus, and restoring focus when the modal closes.

best_practices: Start with the smallest pattern that solves the current UI problem.
Adapt examples to your project state model, routing, and data-fetching library.
Pair performance changes with measurement before and after implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not add complex compound components for one-off UI elements.
Do not memoize every value without a measured rendering problem.
Do not copy examples without adding production error handling and accessibility checks.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides frontend patterns and examples that can guide application code.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The patterns are useful in React and Next.js projects, especially component and performance work.

faq.question: Does it include state management guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes context, reducer, custom hook, and async query patterns.

faq.question: Is it focused on design visuals?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on implementation patterns, accessibility, performance, and maintainable UI structure.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes, but TypeScript and React basics make the examples easier to apply.

faq.question: Does it perform network requests?
faq.answer: No. The skill file only documents frontend examples, including sample data-fetching code.
